postData.contentHtml:
The idea of expanding Mount Rushmore to include a fifth presidential face has been a recurring topic, especially concerning former President Donald Trump, who has reportedly expressed a desire for such an honor. However, this notion recently faced a direct public rebuttal from an unexpected quarter: Fox News.

During a segment, host Brian Kilmeade openly dismissed the suggestion of carving Trump's image onto the iconic South Dakota monument. Kilmeade's stance, described as "breaking rank," resonated with many and fueled discussions online, where sentiment was generally positive though not without debate.

Trump's past visits to Mount Rushmore, including a notable speech, have often reignited speculation about his aspirations for the monument. He has previously shared edited images depicting his face alongside those of George Washington, Thomas Jefferson, Theodore Roosevelt, and Abraham Lincoln.

Kilmeade's comments underscore a moment where even within traditionally supportive media, certain proposals may meet with resistance, highlighting the ongoing public discourse surrounding historical monuments and presidential legacies.
postData.title: Fox News Host Breaks Rank, Dismisses Trump's Mount Rushmore Ambition
postData.summary: In a notable departure from common network sentiment, Fox News host Brian Kilmeade recently rejected the notion of adding Donald Trump's likeness to Mount Rushmore, sparking conversation and debate across social media.

sources.title: ‘Let’s Just Keep The Guys We’ve Got’: Fox News Host Shoots Down Idea of Trump’s Face on Mt. Rushmore
